Oh!, Woe!, (exclamation of pain — indicates desire or uneasiness)
sighing, groaning (expression of grief or physical distress)

1e) (Hophal): to be begun

term_inventory_greek.meaning: overwhelmingly sorrowful; greatly distressing; greatly grieved, exceedingly sorrowful, greatly distressed (Mt. 26:38; Mk. 14:34)
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: perilupos: very sad, deeply grieved; opposed to pericharēs; stugnos kai p. (LXX)

verse_records.verse_text: But the Lord's portion is his people, Jacob his allotted heritage.
the cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
'How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity comes upon them? That God distributes pains in his anger?'
And if they are bound in chains and caught in the cords of affliction,
The cords of death encompassed me; the torrents of destruction assailed me;
the cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
The snares of death encompassed me; the pangs of Sheol laid hold on me; I suffered distress and anguish.
They will be dismayed: pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor. They will look aghast at one another; their faces will be aflame.
Like a pregnant woman who writhes and cries out in her pangs when she is near to giving birth, so were we because of you, O Lord;
'Before she was in labor she gave birth; before her pain came upon her she delivered a son.'
What will you say when they set as head over you those whom you yourself have taught to be friends to you? Will not pangs take hold of you like those of a woman in labor?
O inhabitant of Lebanon, nested among the cedars, how you will be pitied when pangs come upon you, pain as of a woman in labor!
Damascus has become feeble, she turned to flee, and panic seized her; anguish and sorrows have taken hold of her, as of a woman in labor.
The pangs of childbirth come for him, but he is an unwise son, for at the right time he does not present himself at the opening of the womb.
Jabez was more honorable than his brothers; and his mother called his name Jabez, saying, 'Because I bore him in pain.'
And see if there be any grievous way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting!
When the Lord has given you rest from your pain and turmoil and the hard service with which you were made to serve,
I declared them to you from of old, before they came to pass I announced them to you, lest you should say, 'My idol did them, my carved image and my metal image commanded them.'
Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaining? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
For my sighing comes instead of my bread, and my groanings are poured out like water.
'Today also my complaint is bitter; my hand is heavy on account of my groaning.'
I am weary with my moaning; every night I flood my bed with tears; I drench my couch with my weeping.
For my life is spent with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength fails because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
O Lord, all my longing is before you; my sighing is not hidden from you.
Because of my loud groaning my bones cling to my flesh.
A stern vision is told to me; the traitor betrays, and the destroyer destroys. Go up, O Elam; lay siege, O Media; all the sighing she has caused I bring to an end.
And the ransomed of the Lord shall return and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads; they shall obtain gladness and joy, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
And the ransomed of the Lord shall return and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads; they shall obtain gladness and joy, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
You said, 'Woe is me! For the Lord has added sorrow to my pain. I am weary with my groaning, and I find no rest.'
'Let all their evildoing come before you, and deal with them as you have dealt with me because of all my transgressions; for my groans are many, and my heart is faint.'

And among these nations you shall find no respite, and there shall be no resting place for the sole of your foot, but the Lord will give you there a trembling heart and failing eyes and a languishing soul.
Then he said to them, 'My soul is very sorrowful, even to death; remain here, and watch with me.'
And the king was exceedingly sorry, but because of his oaths and his guests he did not want to break his word to her.
And he said to them, 'My soul is very sorrowful, even to death. Remain here and watch.'
But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was extremely rich.
Jesus, seeing that he had become sad, said, 'How difficult it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!'
my eye grows dim through sorrow. Every day I call upon you, O Lord; I spread out my hands to you.

They shall come and sing aloud on the height of Zion, and they shall be radiant over the goodness of the Lord, over the grain, the wine, and the oil, and over the young of the flock and the herd; their life shall be like a watered garden, and they shall languish no more.
For I will satisfy the weary soul, and every languishing soul I will replenish.

then I will do this to you: I will visit you with panic, with wasting disease and fever that consume the eyes and make the heart ache. And you shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.
To Seth also a son was born, and he called his name Enosh. At that time people began to call upon the name of the Lord.
If you make me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of hewn stones, for if you wield your tool on it you profane it.
You shall not give any of your children to offer them to Molech, and so profane the name of your God: I am the Lord.
My companion stretched out his hand against his friends; he violated his covenant.
I will not violate my covenant or alter the word that went forth from my lips.
Therefore I will profane the princes of the sanctuary, and deliver Jacob to utter destruction and Israel to reviling.
But he was pierced for our transgressions; he was crushed for our iniquities; upon him was the chastisement that brought us peace, and with his wounds we are healed.
I will bring the worst of the nations to take possession of their houses. I will put an end to the pride of the strong, and their holy places shall be profaned.

But I acted for the sake of my name, that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations among whom they lived, in whose sight I made myself known to them in bringing them out of the land of Egypt.

And I will vindicate the holiness of my great name, which has been profaned among the nations, and which you have profaned among them. And the nations will know that I am the Lord, declares the Lord God, when through you I vindicate my holiness before their eyes.
Have we not all one Father? Has not one God created us? Why then are we faithless to one another, profaning the covenant of our fathers?
To the woman he said, 'I will surely multiply your pain in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children. Your desire shall be for your husband, and he shall rule over you.'

And to Adam he said, 'Because you have listened to the voice of your wife and have eaten of the tree of which I commanded you, You shall not eat of it, cursed is the ground because of you; in pain you shall eat of it all the days of your life;'
and called his name Noah, saying, 'Out of the ground that the Lord has cursed, this one shall bring us relief from our work and from the painful toil of our hands.'
To the woman he said, 'I will surely multiply your pain in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children. Your desire shall be for your husband, and he shall rule over you.'
It is in vain that you rise up early and go late to rest, eating the bread of anxious toil; for he gives to his beloved sleep.
lest strangers take their fill of your strength, and your labors go to the house of a foreigner,
The blessing of the Lord makes rich, and he adds no sorrow with it.
In all toil there is profit, but mere talk tends only to poverty.
A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
Is this man Coniah a despised, broken pot, a vessel no one cares for? Why are he and his children hurled and cast into a land that they do not know?

Behold, all you who kindle a fire, who equip yourselves with burning torches! Walk by the light of your fire, and by the torches that you have kindled! This you have from my hand: you shall lie down in torment.

cross_registry_links.note: pang (tsir H6735C) appears in sorrow STEP header but is owned by anguish (registry 005). Treated as cross-reference in both parts.
sighing (a.na.chah H0585) has strong overlap with grief vocabulary.
to grieve (lupeō) is cross-referenced to distress (registry 051); perilupos in this part belongs to same LUPE root family as cross-ref terms in prior registries.
perilupos (G4036) belongs to LUPE root family, shared with lupē and lupeō which are cross-referenced to grief (071) and distress (051).

data_quality_flags.description: cha.lal (to profane/begin) has a very wide semantic range (profane, defile, begin). Its inclusion in the sorrow word set appears driven by one specific sense — violation/defilement causing distress — but the majority of its 134 occurrences are not inner-state expressions. Session B should assess whether this term contributes meaningfully to sorrow analysis.

che.vel (pain) has a wide semantic range including cord/rope/territory and pain/pangs. The pain sense (birth pangs, anguish) is a distinct subset. Session B should assess which sense cluster is operative in sorrow context.

e.tsev has only 6 occurrences and Pro 15:1 in the verse set ('A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger') does not appear to use the e.tsev meaning; possible source file error in verse assignment. Session B should verify.
